summary: Android export crash on Android 7.1.2 when entering foreground

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1-stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sonoma (14.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Pro 560X OpenGL Engine - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8850H CPU @ 2.60GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

Start the app, back to home screen and reenter the app, then Crash. My test device is Redmi 5, Android 7.1.2, API 25.

It seems Godot try to relaunch the game. 

Here is the log.

summary: Improve zoom performance in `Script` and `Shader` editors

description.text:
This improves the zoom performance **when you have multiple (previously) opened scripts** in the script/shader text editors. This was done by deferring the expensive `set_zoom_factor` (and subsequent cache invalidation) function calls to only be called when you open a script. 

This was previously updated **N * scroll wheel spins** times for **each script** you had opened; this was done to keep the zoom percentage in sync between scripts, but is now updated and stored in the script/shader editor plugins instead.

> [!WARNING]
>Zooming can still stutter on bigger and/or first-time opened scripts due to cache invalidation within that single code editor instance. Further work could be done to improve this.

summary: Evaluating multiline array erases portions

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Repeatable in v4.4.1.stable.steam [49a5bc7b6] **|**  

_I don't have time to test more or I would_

### System information

v4.4.1.stable.steam [49a5bc7b6]

### Issue description

When creating an array or dictionary, Evaluating the Lines cause them to erase all but one value.

Only if the array / dictionary are on separate lines does this happen, 
When all values are within the same line it is not effected.

### Steps to reproduce

Create an array like this 

`var One_line_Array = {"test": {0: {"test": "test"}, 1: {"test": "test"}}}`

Then move one line down
```
var Two_line_Array = {"test": {0: {"test": "test"}, 
1: {"test": "test"}}}
```

Evaluating this will erase the last portion of the array leaving only

`var Two_line_Array = {"test": {0: {"test": "test"}, 
1`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
This was reproduced in a brand new project

summary: Add property to control showing the virtual keyboard on focus events

description.text:
This PR adds a property to `LineEdit` and `TextEdit` to control whether the virtual keyboard (on platforms that support it) should show on focus events.

The second commit leverages the new property to disable showing the virtual keyboard on focus events for the following editor components:
- Code editor: In the Android editor, this would cause the virtual keyboard to show on start if the script editor was left as the last selected workspace
- Project settings' search bar: Opening the project settings in the Android editor would always show the virtual keyboard regardless of the user's intent
- Editor settings' search: Same behavior as the project settings, this would always cause the virtual keyboard to show regarless of the user's intent

summary: Hexagonal TileMap rendering artifacts: Jagged edges (Linear filter) or Seams/Gaps (Nearest filter) despite texture padding

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono
Godot v4.3.stable.mono
Godot v4.2.2.stable.mono
Godot v4.1.4.stable.mono
Godot v4.0.4.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

Rendering hexagonal tiles (e.g., 32px size) using a TileMapLayer exhibits visual artifacts depending on the texture filtering mode set on the TileMapLayer node.

With the Linear texture filter, the angled top and bottom edges of the hexagonal tiles appear noticeably jagged or have a "sawtooth" pattern (see attached LinearJagged.png).
Switching the TileMap's Texture Filter property to Nearest resolves the jagged edges, but introduces intermittent thin gaps or visible seams between adjacent tiles (see attached NearestJagged.png). These seams often appear or disappear depending on the camera's position and zoom level, suggesting potential floating-point precision issues during rendering.

The Use Texture Padding option is enabled (true) in the TileSet resource for the Atlas source, which should normally mitigate seam issues.



Using Linear filter:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1147bb1a-bd54-4b07-940f-c870f56aeeaf)


Using Nearest filter:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/07134cf2-17a1-410c-b44d-32189598db67)


Attempted Solutions (that did not work or worsened the issue):
1) Ensured Use Texture Padding is enabled in the TileSet.
2) Tested both Linear and Nearest filtering modes on the TileMapLayer (TileMap) node.
3) Enabled Project Settings -> Rendering -> 2D -> Snap -> Snap 2D Transforms To Pixel and Snap 2D Vertices To Pixel. Enabling these settings noticeably worsened the seam artifacts when using Nearest filtering. They were disabled for final testing.
4) Ensured Mipmaps -> Generate was enabled/disabled for the texture atlas upon import.

Expected Result:
Hexagonal tiles should render seamlessly without jagged edges along their borders and without any gaps or seams appearing between adjacent tiles, especially when Use Texture Padding is enabled.

Actual Result:
Linear filtering produces jagged/aliased edges on the non-axis-aligned sides of the hexagons. Nearest filtering removes the jaggedness but introduces intermittent visual gaps/seams between tiles under certain camera positions/zoom levels. Enabling 2D snapping in project settings exacerbated the seam issue with Nearest filtering.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Godot project.
2. Import a texture atlas containing hexagonal tiles (e.g., 32px).
3. Create a main scene with a Node2D.
4. Add a TileMapLayer node as a child. 
5. Create a new TileSet resource for TileMapLayer.
6. Add the imported texture as an Atlas source within the TileSet editor.
7. Configure basic hexagonal tiles from the atlas (Setup tab).
8. In the TileSet Atlas source properties (Select Source tab -> Select the atlas):
Set Tile Shape to Hexagon.
Set Tile Layout appropriately.
Ensure Use Texture Padding is enabled (true).
9. Select the TileMapLayer and paint several adjacent hexagonal tiles onto the map.
10. Create a Camera2D node. Write a script for using it (moving and zooming needed).
11. Run the scene.
12. Observe the tile edges with the default Linear filter on the TileMap (Inspector -> Inherited Texture -> Texture Filter). You should see the jagged edges.
13. Change the Texture Filter property on the TileMap node to Nearest.
14. Move the camera around (e.g., by script or dragging) and change the camera's Zoom property (e.g., to values like 1.1, 1.5, 0.8). Observe the intermittent seams/gaps appearing and disappearing between tiles.

See minimal reproduction projects.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

(for Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono & Godot v4.3.stable.mono)

[GapsBetweenTiles_4.4.1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20047848/GapsBetweenTiles_4.4.1.zip)


(for Godot v4.2.2.stable.mono & Godot v4.3.stable.mono & Godot v4.1.4.stable.mono & Godot v4.0.4.stable.mono)

[GapsBetweenTiles_4.2.2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20047865/GapsBetweenTiles_4.2.2.zip)

summary: Fix edit resource on inspector when inside array or dictionary

description.text:
Fixes #91473 - First PR (first time using C++)

Fixes Inspector not opening Resource from inside Array or Dictionary when
clicking "Edit", by properly signaling `resource_selected`.

When clicking "Edit" on a Resource in the Inspector, the `resource_selected`
signal is emitted. This works correctly when the Resource is a standalone
exported property, and the signal reaches `EditorInspector::_resource_selected`.

However, when the Resource is stored inside an Array or Dictionary, the signal
was not reaching the EditorInspector, because it was blocked by the container
property (Array/Dictionary) in between.

This fix forwards the `resource_selected` signal from the nested Resource
through the container (Array or Dictionary), up to the Inspector, allowing it
to display the selected Resource as expected.

summary: GDScript: Allow export nodes to be dropped in with ALT key

description.text:
Resolves proposal [#11897](https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/discussions/11897).

This allows for the `ALT` or `OPTION (macOS)` key to be used as a way to drop `@export` variables automatically into the GDScript editor. `CTRL` takes precedence, so having both `ALT` and `CTRL` pressed will create an `@onready` variable.

A possible extension of this implementation would be to automatically assign the corresponding node property to all nodes who share the same script in the current scene through the `_find_all_node_for_script` method. However, a large issue is that you would have to wait for the `property_list_changed` signal before actually applying anything, having to connect & disconnect independently for each node. In addition, I also found that setting the properties manually would not actually apply the changes properly, as saving the script/scene would reset these variables back to null. (Some advice as to how this could be implemented would be greatly appreciated 🙏)

summary: Godot 4.3 and 4.4 model importer renames skeleton bones, breaking animation retargeting and bone map support

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.3.0 up to 4.4.1 (newest current version)
- Not reproducible in Godot 4.2.2

### System information

Windows 10, Godot 4.2.2 / 4.3.0 / 4.4.1

### Issue description

I have a library of character models stored as a single GLB file.
The characters have similar but not always identical humanoid skeletons.
I have created bone maps for the humanoid characters but some extra bones remain unmapped.
Since Godot 4.3.0 all bones in the GLB file are treated as belonging to a single skeleton and duplicate names are silently renamed even though there should be no conflict. The new import feature "Import as Skeleton Bones" is explicitly turned off (default).

This is what the bone names on the second skeleton in the GLB look like in Godot 4.2:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c61c806-f60d-4281-a875-08a3765db483)

This is what happens to the skeleton bone names if imported into Godot 4.3 or higher:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/36801aa1-8385-4054-8881-e2fc68597788)

For the MRP example I downloaded free assets from here (only the two characters are included in the MRP):
https://syntystore.com/products/polygon-starter-pack

### Steps to reproduce

I have attached a tiny Godot 4.2 project to demonstrate the issue.
It contains a single GLB file called "Humans.glb".
The GLB contains 2 rigged characters. Each character model has its own mesh and skeleton.
There is also a bone map with a humanoid profile included in the project.
This setup work perfectly in Godot 4.2.2.

Try upgrading the project to Godot 4.3 or higher.
The setup no longer works.
Godot now insists that the bones on the second skeleton have different names with the suffix "_2".
It is no longer possible to import the model into Godot. For example the bone "Eyebrows" will always have different names among skeletons, which breaks animation retargeting.
This is a breaking change but if fails silently so I only realized it's happening just now.

In my opinion the problem is caused by  the new feature "Import as Skeleton Bones" which can't be fully disabled even though it's turned off by default.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[SkeletonBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20027872/SkeletonBug.zip)

description.text:
FIXES: #88119

This can result in multiple gizmos being created for the same nodes in some circumstances

Requests for node gizmos are meant to happen only once, as specified in the [documentation](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.4/classes/class_editornode3dgizmoplugin.html#class-editornode3dgizmoplugin-private-method-has-gizmo) "Whenever a node is added to a scene this method is called".

In practice, it was possible for this to be called multiple times in some circumstances, resulting in multiple gizmos being created for a given node.

To Reproduce Error:
1. Create new 3D scene from FileSystem dock
2. With the scene open, select the created Node3D
3. Save the scene by pressing Ctrl + S
4. Click Scene > Reload Saved Scene
5. (you will now have multiple gizmos requested for the selected Node3D)

It is possible to reproduce this problem in other ways that are more complex and less likely to occur, and more impactful, while harder to debug. (Took me several hours of debugging to narrow it down to a `emit_changed.call_deferred()` being called by a setter function applying initial `@export` values to an instance in the scene - these were then being executed in order, and resulted in many deffered calls to `_request_gizmo` being stacked due to multiple `update_gizmos` calls)

(as an aside, it is likely that repeated and un-needed calls to `_request_gizmo` are being made for nodes that have no applicable gizmos)

For the plugin, the following code shoud suffice for testing/debugging purposes.

```GDScript
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var gizmo_plugin = MyGizmo.new()
func _enter_tree() -> void:
	add_node_3d_gizmo_plugin(gizmo_plugin)
func _exit_tree() -> void:
	remove_node_3d_gizmo_plugin(gizmo_plugin)

class MyGizmo extends EditorNode3DGizmoPlugin:
	func _has_gizmo(for_node_3d: Node3D) -> bool:
		print(for_node_3d)
		return false
```

(note: this is my first ever pull request! sorry if this comes off as strange)

description.text:

### Issue description

When exporting an unsigned integer field in C#, it is possible to assign a negative value to it in the inspector. I expected the inspector wouldn't let me do that, or make all negative values zero. But instead it just accepts it. When the negative value is printed to the console, it converts a value of -1 to 4294967295. There are no errors or warnings for this behaviour.

[Export] uint value;

It is now possible to set the uint value to a negative value in the inspector.

summary: Inspector documentation tooltip hidden by any mouse movement

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.dev3, 4.4.stable.official
- Not in v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8]

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev3 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.5768) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1185G7 @ 3.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

The tooltips that show the documentation in the inspector behave differently to normal tooltips. It seems that *any* mouse movement will cause the tooltip to disappear. This occurs in v4.4 and v4.5.dev3, but doesn't in v4.3.

The expected behaviour (as is typical in other desktop software) is to leave the tooltip visible in its initial position, until the cursor leaves the element that the tooltip belongs to. Most tooltips in Godot exhibit the expected behaviour.

In the attached video I show the behaviour of both "normal" tooltips and documentation tooltips.
The documentation tooltips (shown when the mouse is over a property's name) are hidden as soon as the mouse moves, even though the cursor stays within the bounds of the label.
The "normal" tooltip (shown when the cursor is over the colour) shows the expected behaviour.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dfa792b0-e3a8-48e6-8b29-0b79979165e0

(Yes, this is recorded in my configured daily-use editor. I am confident that the issue is unrelated to my editor settings.)

This issue exacerbates #104205 but they are separate problems.
This issue concerns the *hiding* of the tooltip, not the *showing*.

This may be related to #104672, which also concerns documentation tooltips and appears to have appeared between 4.3 and 4.4.
But, to be clear, the issue I'm reporting involves visible mouse movement, and I don't have a tablet plugged in.

### Steps to reproduce

- Select something editable (so there are properties shown in the inspector)
- Cause a documentation tooltip to appear by hovering the mouse cursor over a property name
  - Move the mouse slightly, keeping the cursor over the label
  - observe the tooltip disappearing
- Cause a "normal" tooltip to appear by hovering the mouse cursor over a numeric field or property group (e.g. `Process`)
  - Move the mouse in any fashion (incl. with reckless abandon), without causing the cursor to exit the element / tooltip trigger region
  - observe the tooltip not disappearing

N/A

summary: Fix Set focus on right control on user action "show in file system"

description.text:
## Problem

Previously, when using the "Show in File System" action (e.g., from the 3D viewport), the target file would be selected in the File System dock, but keyboard focus remained on the dock's header instead of moving to the file list/tree.

This forced an extra manual step: after triggering the action via shortcut, users had to click the file again before they could use shortcuts like "Open in external program" or "Open in terminal".

## Solution

This PR ensures that focus automatically moves to the File System dock's content area (tree/list view) when "Show in File System" is triggered.

But it does this only when calling the `navigate_to_path` method. When calling the internal `_navigate_to_path` method, the behavior remains the same.

## Result

Now, the workflow is seamless:

- Select a resource (e.g., in the 3D viewport).
- Press "Show in File System" shortcut (focus moves to the file list).
- Immediately use "Open in external program" shortcut or other actions without extra clicks.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/215f81b2-cdfa-4414-87b2-7843d7e1c486)

summary: Draft: Add option to generate collision shapes only in scene importer (depends on #77937)

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->

Requires #77937 to be merged first

This PR adds the option to generate CollisionShapes without being restricted to a specific physics body type when importing a scene.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d65038d-8477-4097-83fe-23e5eb739f10

Layer and Mask options are hidden from the inspector when using Collision Only, as those will be determined by the parent body used later.

This will allow reusing the same imported CollisionShapes in both static and dynamic contexts, as you can just instance the same scene.

Currently, you would have to generate the CollisionShapes manually for different usages of the imported scene, and if you make changes to the imported scene meshes, the CollisionShapes will not reflect those changes, and you will need to regenerate them manually again.

summary: Fix TileSet Editor harmless error when switching resources in inspector

description.text:
fixes #106017. switching between resources in the inspector that have a tileset property causes a harmless error to appear: "condition 'tile_set.is_null()' is true". this happens because the drag-and-drop system wrongly triggers _drop_data_fw() with invalid data during the switch.

this pr adds validation inside _drop_data_fw() to check the drag data before doing anything, which prevents the error while keeping all functionality intact. error is gone, everything still works as expected.

summary: SpringBoneSimulator3Ds not working when added through code

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable, 4.5 dev 3

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti Laptop GPU - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-12900H (20 threads)

### Issue description

When attempting to add SpringBoneSimulator3Ds through code (instead of adding them through the editor), the added SpringBoneSimulator3D does not work in-game.
Through testing it in-game and looking at the remote scene tree while the game is running, I believe that the error may come from missing joints (for more information on this process, look below). I expect the SpringBoneSimulator3D added through code to automatically create the joints in the same way as the one created in the editor when both root and end bones are set and are part of the same bone chain, or at least, allow the user to create joints.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19815570-91c9-4057-902d-94ae100f72e3

### Steps to reproduce

Steps using the minimal reproduction project
Open the test scene
There are two basic models of cubes, each with 3 bones. One model has a SpringBoneSimulator3D added through the editor, and the other model will add one at _ready in-game.
Run the test scene and use the arrow keys to move the models around. The left model is the one added through code while the right model is the one added through the editor.
While the game is running, look at the remote scene tree and at the added SpringBoneSimulator3D. It appears that the joints automatically added when both root and end bones are set in the editor are completely missing in the one added through code.

General steps (without using the MRP)
Import a model with a rig (to see the spring bones in action).
Attach a script to add a SpringBoneSimulator3D to the model in-game.
Run the game.
Look at the remote scene tree and at the added SpringBoneSimulator3D's joints.

While mainly tested in version 4.4.1.stable, I have tested it to be also reproducible in the newest dev version as of the time writing this (4.5 dev 3).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[spring-bone-modifier-bug-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20019414/spring-bone-modifier-bug-mrp.zip)

summary: Fix wrong context menu argument from FileSystem's item list

description.text:
EditorContextMenuPlugin is supposed to receive list of paths for FileSystem's callback.
It works when clicking the option, but it's broken when using the shortcut, but only in ItemList. The method was wrongly receiving a list of selected item IDs.

This PR fixes it and improves `_file_list_get_selected()` method.

summary: Fix tree scroll

description.text:
## Summary
Fixes #105967 – `scroll_horizontal_enabled` / `scroll_vertical_enabled` flags were ignored: even when both checkboxes were unchecked, the `Tree` still allowed scrolling and displayed scrollbars.

## Changes
* `update_scrollbars()` – scrollbars are now shown only when `<flag> && <content_exceeds_viewport>`.
* `_gui_input()` – early-return for wheel and drag scrolling if the respective scroll flag is disabled.
* Also fixed a shadowing issue where the flags were overridden in the scrollbar loop.

## Before / After
| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| ![Before-Fix](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b091b3dc-98f3-433a-b023-5681c346abc2) | ![Scroll-Fix](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a0d52e6-6060-427a-95d6-911385602f9c) |

> *In the "Before" version, scrolling still happens even when vertical scrolling is disabled.*  
> *The "After" version correctly disables scrolling and hides the scrollbars.*

_No API changes, no new strings._
